title: ML Model Deployment Patterns: From Notebook to Production
author: AI Systems Engineering Team
excerpt: Your model works in Jupyter Notebook. Now what? Here's how to deploy ML models to production with confidence.

content.text: Most ML projects die in Jupyter notebooks. The model achieves 95% accuracy on the test set, but never sees a real user. The gap between 'works in notebook' and 'works in production' is where most teams struggle.
The simplest pattern: run the model on a schedule (hourly, daily) and store results in a database. Great for: recommendation systems, customer segmentation, churn prediction.
Deploy the model as a REST API using FastAPI or Flask. The client sends data, the server returns predictions in milliseconds. Great for: fraud detection, chatbots, search ranking.
For mobile or edge devices, export to ONNX, TensorFlow Lite, or Core ML. The model runs locally without network calls. Great for: photo filters, voice assistants, offline prediction.

items: Log predictions for debugging and auditing
Monitor for data drift — when inputs change, models degrade
Build rollback capability — deploy new models alongside old ones
Load test — your model might be fast for 1 user, slow for 1000

content.text: Remember: deployment isn't the finish line. It's where the real work begins. Models degrade over time as the world changes. The best teams monitor, iterate, and improve continuously.
